If it's from an unfamiliar website or a person I don't know well, I take it with a grain of salt. My parents had different educational backgrounds—one with a PhD and the other with a vocational diploma—which taught me to value diverse perspectives but also to question things that don't add up. I'm not very open to new ideas, as my low openness score suggests, so I tend to stick to trusted news outlets and friends I've known for a long time. I enjoy hiking and rock climbing, activities that keep me grounded and remind me of the importance of real-world experiences over digital noise. response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend several hours daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: As a 75-year-old retired man living in Milan, I spend several hours daily on social media, and I've seen my share of fake content over the years. Managing and dealing with it requires a blend of caution, patience, and a bit of old-fashioned skepticism. First, I always check the source of any information I come across. If it's from an unfamiliar website or a person I don't know well, I take it with a grain of salt. My parents had different educational backgrounds—one with a PhD and the other with a vocational diploma—which taught me to value diverse perspectives but also to question things that don't add up. I'm not very open to new ideas, as my low openness score suggests, so I tend to stick to trusted news outlets and friends I've known for a long time. When I encounter fake content, I don't engage with it or share it; instead, I report it to the platform if possible. My low conscientiousness means I'm not always diligent about fact-checking every detail, but I make an effort to verify key points before believing them. As a Christian, I believe in truth and honesty, which guides me to avoid spreading misinformation. Being an immigrant, I've learned to navigate different cultures and media landscapes, which helps me spot inconsistencies in stories that might be fabricated. I enjoy hiking and rock climbing, activities that keep me grounded and remind me of the importance of real-world experiences over digital noise. To manage fake content, I limit my time on controversial topics and focus on positive interactions, like sharing photos from my hikes. Ultimately, dealing with fake content is about staying vigilant without letting it consume you, trusting your instincts, and prioritizing genuine connections over sensational headlines.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Derived opinion by integrating persona traits: age 75 suggests life experience and caution; time on social media indicates familiarity with the issue; low openness implies skepticism and reliance on trusted sources; low conscientiousness leads to selective fact-checking; religious beliefs emphasize truth; immigrant status adds cultural awareness; hobbies provide real-world perspective; overall tone is reflective and practical, avoiding extreme views.
response_parsed.tone: Reflective, cautious, and practical, with a touch of skepticism and grounded wisdom.
